title: The One With The Engagement Picture

jokes.text: Someone on the subway licked my neck! Licked my neck!
jokes.setup: Someone asking what's wrong, expecting a normal complaint
jokes.punchline: Absurd and gross subway encounter - someone licking a stranger's neck
jokes.explanation: Strong absurdist humor with good character fit for Phoebe's weird NYC encounters. The repetition adds emphasis and disgust.

jokes.text: Whoa! Willy's still alive.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's neck-licking story
jokes.punchline: Joey recognizes the neck-licker as someone he knows named Willy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - casual recognition of a subway neck-licker as an acquaintance. Great character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Ooh, I'm afraid that does not exist.
jokes.setup: Monica saying they're looking for a good picture of her and Chandler
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately shoots down the possibility
jokes.explanation: Good sibling ribbing from Ross, delivered with characteristic bluntness.

jokes.text: No, there are great pictures of you next to a guy who's going like this...
jokes.setup: Monica defending their photos
jokes.punchline: Chandler admits he makes terrible faces in photos while presumably demonstrating
jokes.explanation: Self-aware Chandler humor with visual component. Sets up the photo storyline nicely.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, that's the creep that you're with at the Statue of Liberty.
jokes.setup: Looking at photos together
jokes.punchline: Monica calling her own fiancé a creep in the photo
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of the bad photo theme, Monica's brutal honesty about Chandler's appearance.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm not in that. - No, but look at me, all tan.
jokes.setup: Chandler pointing out a 'great' photo
jokes.punchline: It's great because Monica looks tan, not because it's a good couples photo
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection - sets up expectation of good couple photo, reveals Monica's vanity instead.

jokes.text: [WHOOSHING] Yeah, that's great. Next to that, Chandler won't look so stupid.
jokes.setup: Discussing getting professional photos with wind machines
jokes.punchline: Monica making whooshing sounds then insulting Chandler's looks
jokes.explanation: Monica's characteristic bluntness with physical comedy element. Continues the theme.

jokes.text: All right, but I should warn you. I'm not going. I'm going.
jokes.setup: Chandler agreeing to professional photos
jokes.punchline: Classic Chandler flip-flop delivered in his signature style
jokes.explanation: Pure Chandler - the nervous flip-flopping is perfectly in character and well-executed.

jokes.text: And those guys were this close to letting us play this time too.
jokes.setup: Ross and Joey discussing basketball highlights
jokes.punchline: Reveals they weren't actually playing, just watching and hoping to join
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of their basketball prowess talk - reveals their outsider status.

jokes.text: You call him 'Cute Coffeehouse Guy'? We call him 'Hums While He Pees.'
jokes.setup: Different nickname for the same person
jokes.punchline: Joey's group has a much more specific and gross nickname
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between romantic and gross nicknames. Highly quotable and surprisingly specific detail.

jokes.text: Yes, and we call Ross 'Lingers in the Bathroom.'
jokes.setup: Just established their bathroom-related nickname system
jokes.punchline: Ross gets an equally embarrassing bathroom nickname
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that includes Ross. The nickname system is funny and specific.

jokes.text: 'Hums While He Pees' just asked me out.
jokes.setup: Just learned his gross nickname
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately adopts the nickname while announcing her date
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - she embraces the gross nickname without judgment.

jokes.text: Ross, maybe you know him.
jokes.setup: Guy is getting divorced
jokes.punchline: Monica suggests Ross knows him, implying divorced men form a group
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Ross's defensive response. Monica's casual cruelty about his divorce history.

jokes.text: It's not a club.
jokes.setup: Monica's implication that divorced men know each other
jokes.punchline: Ross's defensive clarification that it's not an organized group
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross defensiveness with great economy. Sets up the divorced men's club running gag.

jokes.text: They have that that on the napkins at the club.
jokes.setup: Ross defending divorced men, then denying there's a club
jokes.punchline: Immediately references the club's napkins, contradicting himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-contradiction that reveals there IS a club. Great character moment for Ross.

jokes.text: Ugh, I could just spread him on a cracker.
jokes.setup: Talking about her attractive assistant Tag doing sit-ups
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific food metaphor for sexual attraction
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and unexpected metaphor. Very quotable Rachel moment with perfect absurdity.

jokes.text: Soon he'll be able to call you 'that lady he knew that got fired.'
jokes.setup: Rachel mentioning Tag calling her his 'work-wife'
jokes.punchline: Monica predicts the workplace crush will get Rachel fired
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Monica showing the potential consequences. Realistic workplace humor.

jokes.text: Why, is he? He is, isn't he? He's dating that slut in marketing.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if Rachel would mind Tag dating someone
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately spirals into jealous assumptions
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's insecurity and jealousy spiral. Believable workplace dynamics.

jokes.text: Maybe I should open a divorced men's club. - Dude, that's so sad.
jokes.setup: Continuing the divorced men theme
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt assessment that the club idea is pathetic
jokes.explanation: Joey's characteristic bluntness deflates Ross's idea. Good friend dynamic.

jokes.text: I could put a basketball court in the back. - Could I play?
jokes.setup: Ross trying to make his sad divorced club sound appealing
jokes.punchline: Chandler wants to join despite not being divorced
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the basketball outsider theme. Chandler's social desperation is funny.

jokes.text: Oh, well, drop and give me 10 more. Heh.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking Tag about his sit-ups
jokes.punchline: Rachel making an inappropriate military-style command to her subordinate
jokes.explanation: Workplace inappropriate behavior played for cringe. Shows Rachel's lack of boundaries.

jokes.text: I had a drink with lunch.
jokes.setup: Tag confused by Rachel's inappropriate command
jokes.punchline: Rachel blaming alcohol for her behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's awkward attempt to cover her inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: [CLEARS THROAT] ... Huh. ... Ah.
jokes.setup: Rachel alone after the awkward Tag interaction
jokes.punchline: Series of throat clearing and sighs showing her internal cringe
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing Rachel's embarrassment. Simple but effective character beat.

jokes.text: Okay, whoa, whoa. Easy there, Melissa. This ain't a locker room, okay.
jokes.setup: Melissa being frank about wanting to sleep with Tag
jokes.punchline: Rachel's hypocritical reaction to sexual frankness at work
jokes.explanation: Good hypocrisy from Rachel who just acted inappropriately herself. Character-driven workplace humor.

jokes.text: Yeah, Melissa, I don't want to be known as the, uh, office bitch... but I will call your supervisor.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to get rid of competition for Tag
jokes.punchline: Rachel threatening to tattle while claiming she doesn't want to be the office bitch
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rachel moment - doing exactly what she claims she doesn't want to do. Good escalation of her jealousy.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, is the seat uncomfortable? - No, I am.
jokes.setup: Photographer asking about physical discomfort
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-aware admission of being uncomfortable as a person
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler self-awareness. Perfect economy and structure. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Try looking sexy. - Okay. ... Or not.
jokes.setup: Photographer asking Chandler to look sexy
jokes.punchline: Chandler's attempt is so bad the photographer immediately gives up
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy premise. The photographer's quick retreat sells Chandler's bad sexy face.

jokes.text: You got anything that's not Ralph Lauren? - Yeah, I don't think so, Joe.
jokes.setup: Joey borrowing a shirt from Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler only owns designer clothes, Joey wants something more normal
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Chandler's wardrobe and class differences between the friends.

jokes.text: It's gonna take a lot of money for me to go out on a date with a dude.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking Joey to take out Tag
jokes.punchline: Joey misunderstanding this as a romantic date request
jokes.explanation: Good Joey misunderstanding with characteristic bluntness. Sets up the clarification nicely.

jokes.text: Really? Because I could kind of use the money.
jokes.setup: Rachel clarifying she's not asking for a romantic date
jokes.punchline: Joey expressing willingness to go on a date with a man for money
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment showing his financial desperation and casual attitude toward sexuality.

jokes.text: This picture's supposed to say 'Geller and Bing to be married'... not 'Local woman saves drowning moron.'
jokes.setup: Monica frustrated with Chandler's photo faces
jokes.punchline: Specific newspaper headline describing how bad Chandler looks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific visual description. Very quotable and captures Monica's frustrated precision. Great escalation.

jokes.text: He's my drowning moron.
jokes.setup: People laughing at Monica's insult about Chandler
jokes.punchline: Monica defending him with the same insult as an endearment
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback that shows Monica's protective nature. Great economy and relationship humor.

jokes.text: It seems to say, 'I love you and that's why I have to kill you.'
jokes.setup: Looking at another photo
jokes.punchline: Monica's interpretation of Chandler's expression as murderous love
jokes.explanation: Dark and specific description that's very quotable. Good escalation of photo critique.

jokes.text: Why did you ever sleep with me?
jokes.setup: Chandler seeing his terrible bedroom eyes
jokes.punchline: Chandler questioning Monica's past attraction to him
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating Chandler moment with relationship callback. Natural escalation from photo theme.

jokes.text: Do you really want to pull at that thread?
jokes.setup: Chandler asking why Monica was attracted to him
jokes.punchline: Monica warning that examining their attraction might unravel everything
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor and relationship humor. Implies their attraction is fragile but they're committed anyway.

jokes.text: Oh! No, that's okay. He's a friend.
jokes.setup: Kyle apologizing for the subway neck-licker
jokes.punchline: Phoebe casually reveals the neck-licker is her friend
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - only she would be friends with a subway neck-licker. Great callback with escalation.

jokes.text: Well, I missed the semifinals, so I think I would just be lost.
jokes.setup: Kyle inviting her to ballroom dancing finals
jokes.punchline: Phoebe treating it like a TV show where missing episodes matters
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe logic - treating live competition like serialized entertainment. Very character-specific absurdity.

jokes.text: My ex-wife Whitney is out there. I cannot deal with her now. That woman is crazy.
jokes.setup: Kyle seeming normal and reasonable
jokes.punchline: Reveals his own issues with ex-wife drama
jokes.explanation: Sets up the ex-wife subplot. Standard divorced person exposition but necessary for plot.

jokes.text: All right, I'll do it. But just because you're a friend.
jokes.setup: Ross refusing to help with ex-wife diversion
jokes.punchline: Immediately changes mind when offered ballroom dancing tickets
jokes.explanation: Good Ross moment showing his priorities and social awkwardness. Sets up the diversion plot.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, but you'd better go, Pheebs. - Okay, Sherlock.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to get rid of Phoebe because his ex is there
jokes.punchline: Ginger's sarcastic response calling him Sherlock
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response. Establishes Ginger as sharp-tongued but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Kyle's ex-wife? You were supposed to divert her, not date her.
jokes.setup: Ross was meant to distract the ex-wife
jokes.punchline: He ended up dating her instead
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the diversion plan gone wrong. Sets up the relationship conflict nicely.

jokes.text: That's where we realized we are both super cool people.
jokes.setup: Ross defending going to ballroom dancing
jokes.punchline: Ross calling himself super cool for enjoying ballroom dancing
jokes.explanation: Good Ross moment - his complete lack of coolness awareness while claiming to be super cool.

jokes.text: And she uses sex as a weapon.
jokes.setup: Phoebe listing Whitney's bad qualities
jokes.punchline: Escalates to bizarre specific accusation about weaponizing sex
jokes.explanation: Very quotable specific accusation. The escalation from paint colors to sex weapons is absurd.

jokes.text: At breakfast, I'll be on full alert for room painting and sex weapons.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's warnings about Whitney
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically promising to watch for these specific weird behaviors
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic Ross response. The combination of mundane and bizarre is very quotable.

jokes.text: Uh, your door isn't soundproof.
jokes.setup: Ross and Phoebe arguing loudly about Whitney
jokes.punchline: Whitney revealing she heard herself being called crazy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Simple but effective escalation of the awkward situation.

jokes.text: You see? Nothing is good enough for her.
jokes.setup: Whitney pointing out the door isn't soundproof
jokes.punchline: Phoebe using this reasonable complaint as evidence Whitney's impossible to please
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe logic - using Whitney's reasonable complaint as evidence of her unreasonableness. Great escalation.

jokes.text: Women? You mean, like old women? - Kind of old, like 30.
jokes.setup: Tag talking about meeting women
jokes.punchline: Tag calling 30-year-olds 'kind of old' to Rachel who's around 30
jokes.explanation: Good age-related humor that hits Rachel where it hurts. Shows Tag's youth and obliviousness.

jokes.text: How you doing?
jokes.setup: Joey teaching Tag his pickup techniques
jokes.punchline: Tag using Joey's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey callback. Shows Tag learning from the master. Very quotable signature phrase.

jokes.text: Borrow money from me?
jokes.setup: Joey offering to teach Chandler about photo techniques
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic reference to Joey always borrowing money
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler snark about their friendship dynamic. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Why is there jelly on your shoe?
jokes.setup: Joey teaching photo technique about looking down
jokes.punchline: Chandler gets distracted by Joey's messy eating habits
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's messiness disrupting Chandler's photo lesson.

jokes.text: That's why I never had any luck with Chandler.
jokes.setup: Joey saying you can't teach someone to be good with women
jokes.punchline: Joey citing Chandler as his teaching failure
jokes.explanation: Good Joey insensitivity. Shows their friendship dynamic and Chandler's romantic struggles.

jokes.text: I'm right here.
jokes.setup: Joey talking about Chandler's romantic failures in front of him
jokes.punchline: Chandler's simple acknowledgment of being present for the insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan response. Great economy and timing. Classic 'invisible friend' moment.

jokes.text: Finally!
jokes.setup: Rachel asking Joey not to corrupt Tag
jokes.punchline: Joey's excitement about having a wingman
jokes.explanation: Good Joey enthusiasm. Shows his loneliness and excitement about having a successful wingman.

jokes.text: I'm still right here.
jokes.setup: More conversation about romance happening around Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler repeating his earlier complaint about being ignored
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalation. Shows Chandler's continued invisibility in the conversation.

jokes.text: Hell, I had a crush on you when I met you.
jokes.setup: Rachel describing her crushes as temporary
jokes.punchline: Reveals she had a crush on Joey when they met
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation that makes sense. Shows Rachel's pattern of workplace crushes.

jokes.text: Did you have a crush on me when you first met me? - Yeah, sure.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking if Joey had a crush on her
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual, unenthusiastic confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good Joey casualness about romance. His indifferent confirmation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Can you people not see me?
jokes.setup: Continued conversation about crushes happening around Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler's exasperated plea about being ignored
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Chandler's invisibility. Very quotable and shows his breaking point.

jokes.text: No, 10. You said 10. You can't take that back.
jokes.setup: Rachel offering shirts as payment
jokes.punchline: Joey insisting on the original higher offer like a legal contract
jokes.explanation: Good Joey greed and literal-mindedness. Shows his opportunistic nature and simple logic.

jokes.text: Really? - Yeah, for you.
jokes.setup: Joey saying there's such a thing as too many women
jokes.punchline: Joey clarifying it's only a problem for Tag, not him
jokes.explanation: Good Joey selfishness. Perfect misdirection showing he's not giving up women himself.

jokes.text: Then I fell asleep on the subway and went to Brooklyn. Brooklyn is far.
jokes.setup: Chandler took antihistamines to relax
jokes.punchline: Ended up unconscious on the subway going to Brooklyn
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Joey's bad advice. Chandler's simple observation about Brooklyn being far is endearing.

jokes.text: Hi. - So how are things going with Crazy? Has she cooked your rabbit yet?
jokes.setup: Ross greeting Phoebe after dating Whitney
jokes.punchline: Phoebe referencing 'Fatal Attraction' movie
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference to Fatal Attraction. Works for the character dynamic but not particularly original.

jokes.text: And his response to each one was, 'I don't give a tiny rat's ass.'
jokes.setup: Ross defending Whitney's paint sample behavior
jokes.punchline: Quotes Kyle's dismissive and vulgar response
jokes.explanation: Very quotable specific phrase. Good escalation showing the other side of the story.

jokes.text: Maybe he wouldn't be... if she didn't bring the office home every night.
jokes.setup: Defending Whitney against accusations of bedroom problems
jokes.punchline: Ross getting drawn into their marital argument
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross getting sucked into their relationship drama. Good escalation of the proxy argument.

jokes.text: Well, she certainly knew what she was doing New Year's Eve 1997.
jokes.setup: Argument about Whitney's work dedication
jokes.punchline: Phoebe brings up a very specific past incident
jokes.explanation: Very specific detail that escalates the argument. The precision of the date makes it funnier.

jokes.text: We want the last six years back! - So do we! So do we!
jokes.setup: Proxy argument escalating
jokes.punchline: Both sides shouting about wanting time back
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how they've become the divorced couple. The desperation is funny.

jokes.text: Another night of bird-dogging the chicas?
jokes.setup: Rachel asking about Tag's night with Joey
jokes.punchline: Rachel using Joey-style slang to describe picking up women
jokes.explanation: Good Rachel attempt to sound casual about dating. The slang feels forced which is the point.

jokes.text: I would love to! - What?
jokes.setup: Tag saying he should do something about finding the right person
jokes.punchline: Rachel misunderstands and enthusiastically agrees to be that person
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and cringe moment. Shows Rachel's wishful thinking and creates great awkwardness.

jokes.text: And speaking of being selfish in bed, how's Whitney?
jokes.setup: Someone complaining about selfishness
jokes.punchline: Phoebe transitions to attacking Whitney's bedroom behavior
jokes.explanation: Good aggressive topic transition by Phoebe. Continues the proxy fight effectively.

jokes.text: For the most part.
jokes.setup: Ross defending his bedroom knowledge
jokes.punchline: Admits partial ignorance with understated qualifier
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross admission of partial failure. The understated delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, just say it, Kyle!
jokes.setup: Kyle hesitating to announce their reunion
jokes.punchline: Whitney's impatience with his reluctance
jokes.explanation: Shows Whitney's controlling nature that was described earlier. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: You know, he hums when he pees. - I do know.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to insult Kyle
jokes.punchline: Whitney already knows about his bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the opening nickname. Shows Whitney accepts Kyle's quirks.

jokes.text: Makes him miss the bowl, but whatever.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Kyle's bathroom humming
jokes.punchline: Whitney reveals the gross practical consequence
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with specific gross detail. Very quotable and shows Whitney's blunt honesty.

jokes.text: Yeah, and I don't even give a tiny rat's ass.
jokes.setup: Ross using Kyle's phrase earlier
jokes.punchline: Phoebe adopting the same dismissive phrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the memorable phrase. Shows how the proxy fight adopted their language.

jokes.text: You know, she did try to use sex as a weapon. - Yeah, I hurt my back a little.
jokes.setup: Ross admitting Phoebe was right about Whitney
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was literally injured by the 'sex weapon'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literal interpretation of 'sex as weapon' metaphor. Perfect Ross physical comedy admission. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You know, he hums while he does other stuff too.
jokes.setup: Earlier discussion of Kyle humming while peeing
jokes.punchline: Phoebe revealing Kyle hums during other activities too
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the humming theme with sexual implication. Natural character beat for Phoebe.

jokes.text: Divorced men's club. - Potato, 'potato.'
jokes.setup: Ross correcting Phoebe's 'sad men's club'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe saying they're the same thing with potato idiom
jokes.explanation: Good use of the 'tomato/potato' idiom to dismiss Ross's distinction. Works well for Phoebe's direct style.

jokes.text: Imagine what our kids would look like. - You know, we don't have to imagine.
jokes.setup: Monica wondering about their future children's appearance
jokes.punchline: Chandler pointing out they're getting married so they'll find out
jokes.explanation: Sweet romantic moment with logical Chandler response. Good relationship humor that's optimistic.

jokes.text: I'm marrying her. - We'll just see.
jokes.setup: Chandler stating they're definitely getting married
jokes.punchline: Monica expressing doubt about their future
jokes.explanation: Great ending beat showing Monica's competitive/controlling nature even about marriage. Perfect character moment.
